action.hypothesis: On the directly compatible `topk` line, increasing `K` from 96 to 160 should produce a materially better `<0.5x` Pareto point, likely around the low-0.18 FVU range at about 0.41x total_cost, while avoiding the recent `factorized_topk` pattern of modest quality gains paired with worse throughput.
action.summary: Use `topk` as the next low-cost mainline probe, anchored to r242 and changing only `K` to 160. Final reconstruction formula remains `x_hat = Σ_{i in TopK(x)} α_i(x) b_i`, where all `b_i` come from one static vector library, so this family is `直接兼容` with the LUTurbo/Lottable `查表 + 加权求和 + 选择性补偿` path. This is a strict single-variable step inside the target `<0.5x` region: estimated selection cost stays at the EF=1 topk baseline, deployment lookup rises with `K`, and combined cost should land near 0.41x, filling the gap between the existing 0.38x/0.1968 and 0.44x/0.1635 topk points. Recent `factorized_topk` rounds improved FVU only slightly while increasing runtime cost, so the cleaner frontier-building move is to continue the already monotone-improving topk K ladder.
action.self_review: This is intentionally conservative: it does not open a new family, and it relies on the existing topk trend rather than a new mechanism. The tradeoff is that it will not teach much about new structure, but that is acceptable here because the current priority is to strengthen the `<0.5x` Pareto frontier with a deployable, directly compatible point and to avoid recipes that slow training without a commensurate FVU gain.
notes_to_memory: Recent `factorized_topk` rounds remained far above the best low-cost topk points, and the wider scorer pass at r249 also reduced throughput to about 111k tok/s, so further factorized widening is currently a weak cost-quality bet.
The EF=1 `topk` K ladder has shown consistent FVU improvement from K=96 to K=128 to K=192 without any compatibility risk; an untested midpoint K=160 is the cleanest next `<0.5x` frontier probe.
next_hypotheses: If `topk` K=160 lands near the expected ~0.18 FVU at ~0.41x, test `K=176` next to refine the mid-right edge of the `<0.5x` frontier.
If `topk` K=160 underperforms the monotone trend, shift the next round to a different directly compatible low-cost selector family instead of continuing dense K sweeps.
If runtime regresses disproportionately at K=160, treat that as a system-side warning and prefer lower-cost frontier densification over larger-K topk pushes.
